main_sections.title: The Wise Parrot
main_sections.prose_summary: In a quiet village, a rich merchant keeps a bright, talkative parrot in a golden cage. The parrot entertains the merchant with stories and songs, but secretly longs for freedom. When the merchant prepares to travel, he asks the parrot what gift to bring. The parrot asks for freedom; the merchant refuses. The parrot then requests him to convey greetings to its wild parrot friends in the jungle. During his travels, the merchant finds a flock of parrots and delivers the message. Shockingly, one parrot falls from the tree like a stone. Distressed, the merchant hurries home and narrates the incident to his pet. As soon as he finishes, his own parrot also drops to the cage floor, seemingly dead. Thinking his beloved pet has died, the merchant gently lifts the body and places it on the windowsill. The moment he steps back, the parrot stirs, spreads its wings, and flies away — revealing the whole act was a clever ruse: the wild parrot had sent a message in code, teaching the caged parrot to play dead so the merchant would remove it from the cage. The story celebrates wit and the yearning for freedom over material comfort, with the theme that true intelligence finds a way.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Answer the following questions.
1. Why did the merchant keep the parrot in a cage of gold?
2. What made the parrot a favourite pet of the merchant?
3. What message did the parrot ask the merchant to convey to its friends?
4. What happened after the merchant gave the message to the parrots?
5. How did the parrot escape from the cage?

question_bank.stem: Why did the merchant keep the parrot in a cage of gold?
What made the parrot a favourite pet of the merchant?
What message did the parrot ask the merchant to convey to its friends?
What happened after the merchant gave the message to the parrots?
How did the parrot escape from the cage?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think B + Let us Listen + Let us Speak A + Let us Speak B

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Think and discuss.
1. If you were the merchant, how would you respond to the parrot's request for freedom?
2. Can you think of a real-life situation where intelligence is more useful than strength?
3. Which parrot from the chapter was actually 'wise' according to you? Give reasons for your answer.

question_bank.stem: If you were the merchant, how would you respond to the parrot's request for freedom?
Can you think of a real-life situation where intelligence is more useful than strength?
Which parrot from the chapter was actually 'wise' according to you? Give reasons for your answer.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Listen. Your teacher will read the paragraph given below. Listen to it carefully. Your teacher will ask you some questions, which you can try to answer.
[Paragraph]: Birds are beautiful creatures with feathers and wings. Due to various reasons, many species of birds have become endangered. To protect these birds and their species, bird sanctuaries have been built. There are more than 70 bird sanctuaries in India. The Vedanthangal Bird Sanctuary, located in Tamil Nadu, is the oldest bird sanctuary in India. The Bharatpur Bird sanctuary, located in Rajasthan, is the largest bird sanctuary in our country.
tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: Birds are beautiful creatures with feathers and wings. Due to various reasons, many species of birds have become endangered. To protect these birds and their species, bird sanctuaries have been built. There are more than 70 bird sanctuaries in India. The Vedanthangal Bird Sanctuary, located in Tamil Nadu, is the oldest bird sanctuary in India. The Bharatpur Bird sanctuary, located in Rajasthan, is the largest bird sanctuary in our country. Teacher asks factual questions orally after reading.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Speak A. Listen and repeat the following word pairs, paying attention to the difference between the 'ai' and 'oi' sounds.
tail — toil
bail — boil
ail — oil
fail — foil
cane — coin
sail — soil
Let us Speak B. Look at the pictures below. There are two birds — one in a cage and one flying free. Which bird would you want to be and why? Share your views with the class.

question_bank.stem: Which bird would you want to be — the caged parrot or the free parrot — and why?

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Two illustrations side by side: a green parrot inside a cage, and the same parrot flying freely in the open sky.

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn A + Let us Learn B + Let us Learn C

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn A. Fill in the blanks with suitable phrases given in the box to make a meaningful paragraph.
[Word bank: stand upright, all over, look after, stepped out, went away, came across, about to]
One winter morning, as I ________ of my home, I saw that the snow was ________ the place. While walking uphill towards my shop, I ________ a small puppy. Its leg was injured and it was ________ collapse. Being in pain it was unable to ________. So, I decided to take it home and ________ it. With good care, all its pain ________.

question_bank.stem: As I ________ of my home (stood outside)
the snow was ________ the place
I ________ a small puppy (met/found)
it was ________ collapse
it was unable to ________ (stand straight)
I decided to ________ it (care for it)
all its pain ________ (disappeared)

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn B. Complete the sentences using the correct collective noun. Word bank: pack, bouquet, army, flock, bunch, flight, swarm.
1. A ________ of parrots came to the forest. (flight — given)
2. I saw a ________ of sheep in the fields.
3. A ________ of wolves was howling loudly.
4. We gave a ________ of flowers to the chief guest.
5. A ________ of bees hovered around the flowers.
6. An ________ of ants is moving towards the anthill.
7. I helped my mother find a ________ of keys.

question_bank.stem: I saw a ________ of sheep in the fields.
A ________ of wolves was howling loudly.
We gave a ________ of flowers to the chief guest.
A ________ of bees hovered around the flowers.
An ________ of ants is moving towards the anthill.
I helped my mother find a ________ of keys.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn C. Read the following examples of present perfect tense.
1. 'I have come from Jalandhar.' (action completed in the recent past)
2. 'Rita has read all the poems written by Sarojini Naidu.' (past experience still relevant to present)
Rule: Subject + has/have + Verb + Object
Now complete the following sentences using present perfect tense.
a. I ________ (visit) Delhi many times. It is a city with many historical sites.
b. My friend Onshangla is an artist. She ________ (draw) pictures of ten monuments.
c. Mr. Joon ________ (buy) a new car.
d. I ________ (complete) my homework and my brother ________ (revise) for his test.
e. They ________ (write) letters to their friends.

question_bank.stem: I ________ (visit) Delhi many times.
She ________ (draw) pictures of ten monuments.
Mr. Joon ________ (buy) a new car.
I ________ (complete) my homework and my brother ________ (revise) for his test.
They ________ (write) letters to their friends.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Write. In the story, you read how the parrot tricked the merchant into releasing it but the story does not end here. Use your imagination to complete the parrot's story.
After escaping from the golden cage, the parrot decided to fly away…

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Let us Explore

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Do. A bird feeder is an object used to keep food for birds. They are filled with seeds, grains, or nuts and placed in safe locations to help birds find food easily.
1. Look at the pictures of bird feeders given below and try to identify the material used to make them. [Six types of feeders shown: plastic bottle, coconut shell, bamboo log, ice-cream stick box, clay bowl, juice carton.]
2. Make a bird feeder with the material of your choice. Fill it up with water, seeds, and grains like millets. With the help of your parents, place it at an appropriate location outside your house.

question_bank.stem: Look at the six pictures of bird feeders and identify the material used to make each one.
Make a bird feeder with the material of your choice, fill it with water/seeds/grains like millets, and place it outside your house with parental help.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Six pictures of different homemade and store-bought bird feeders: plastic bottle feeder, coconut shell feeder, bamboo log feeder, ice-cream stick box feeder, clay bowl feeder, and juice carton feeder.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Explore. Look at the pictures of the following ten birds: parrot, macaw, sparrow, crow, ostrich, seagull, pigeon, flamingo, weaver bird, tailor bird. Choose any two of the above and find out a few interesting facts about them.

question_bank.stem: Choose any two birds from the list (parrot, macaw, sparrow, crow, ostrich, seagull, pigeon, flamingo, weaver bird, tailor bird) and find out a few interesting facts about each.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Labelled illustrations of ten birds: parrot, macaw, sparrow, crow, ostrich, seagull, pigeon, flamingo, weaver bird, and tailor bird.
